TFM melanoma - Kaplan-Meier por estadio en TCGA-SKCM

Sonia

2026-03-06

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = TRUE
)

1 1. Instalación de paquetes

if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("BiocManager")
}

paquetes_bioc <- c("TCGAbiolinks", "SummarizedExperiment")
paquetes_cran <- c("dplyr", "tibble", "readr", "stringr", "ggplot2", "survival", "survminer")

for (p in paquetes_bioc) {
  if (!requireNamespace(p, quietly = TRUE)) {
    BiocManager::install(p, ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)
  }
}

for (p in paquetes_cran) {
  if (!requireNamespace(p, quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages(p)
  }
}

2 2. Carga de librerías

library(TCGAbiolinks)
library(SummarizedExperiment)
## Cargando paquete requerido: MatrixGenerics
## Cargando paquete requerido: matrixStats
## 
## Adjuntando el paquete: 'MatrixGenerics'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:matrixStats':
## 
##     colAlls, colAnyNAs, colAnys, colAvgsPerRowSet, colCollapse,
##     colCounts, colCummaxs, colCummins, colCumprods, colCumsums,
##     colDiffs, colIQRDiffs, colIQRs, colLogSumExps, colMadDiffs,
##     colMads, colMaxs, colMeans2, colMedians, colMins, colOrderStats,
##     colProds, colQuantiles, colRanges, colRanks, colSdDiffs, colSds,
##     colSums2, colTabulates, colVarDiffs, colVars, colWeightedMads,
##     colWeightedMeans, colWeightedMedians, colWeightedSds,
##     colWeightedVars, rowAlls, rowAnyNAs, rowAnys, rowAvgsPerColSet,
##     rowCollapse, rowCounts, rowCummaxs, rowCummins, rowCumprods,
##     rowCumsums, rowDiffs, rowIQRDiffs, rowIQRs, rowLogSumExps,
##     rowMadDiffs, rowMads, rowMaxs, rowMeans2, rowMedians, rowMins,
##     rowOrderStats, rowProds, rowQuantiles, rowRanges, rowRanks,
##     rowSdDiffs, rowSds, rowSums2, rowTabulates, rowVarDiffs, rowVars,
##     rowWeightedMads, rowWeightedMeans, rowWeightedMedians,
##     rowWeightedSds, rowWeightedVars

library(dplyr)
## 
## Adjuntando el paquete: 'dplyr'
## The following object is masked from 'package:Biobase':
## 
##     combine
## The following objects are masked from 'package:GenomicRanges':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, union
## The following object is masked from 'package:GenomeInfoDb':
## 
##     intersect
## The following objects are masked from 'package:IRanges':
## 
##     collapse, desc, intersect, setdiff, slice, union
## The following objects are masked from 'package:S4Vectors':
## 
##     first, intersect, rename, setdiff, setequal, union

## 
##     combine, intersect, setdiff, setequal, union

##

## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(tibble)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(survival)
library(survminer)
## Cargando paquete requerido: ggpubr
## 
## Adjuntando el paquete: 'survminer'
## The following object is masked from 'package:survival':
## 
##     myeloma

3 3. Creación de carpetas de trabajo

dir.create("data_raw", showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create("data_processed", showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create("results", showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create("figures", showWarnings = FALSE)

4 4. Descarga de datos clínicos

clinical_tcga <- GDCquery_clinic(
  project = "TCGA-SKCM",
  type = "clinical"
)

dim(clinical_tcga)
## [1] 470  90
write_csv(clinical_tcga, "data_raw/tcga_skcm_clinical.csv")

5 5. Preparación básica de variables clínicas

clinical_tcga$gender <- factor(clinical_tcga$gender)
clinical_tcga$vital_status <- factor(clinical_tcga$vital_status)
clinical_tcga$ajcc_pathologic_stage <- factor(clinical_tcga$ajcc_pathologic_stage)

6 6. Creación de variables de supervivencia

6.1 6.1 Evento de supervivencia global

clinical_tcga <- clinical_tcga %>%
  mutate(
    OS_event = case_when(
      vital_status == "Dead"  ~ 1,
      vital_status == "Alive" ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

table(clinical_tcga$OS_event, useNA = "ifany")
## 
##   0   1 
## 247 223

6.2 6.2 Tiempo de supervivencia global

clinical_tcga <- clinical_tcga %>%
  mutate(
    OS_time = case_when(
      OS_event == 1 ~ as.numeric(days_to_death),
      OS_event == 0 ~ as.numeric(days_to_last_follow_up),
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

summary(clinical_tcga$OS_time)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.    NA's 
##      -2     486    1109    1834    2365   11252       9

6.3 6.3 Filtrado de tiempos válidos

clinical_surv <- clinical_tcga %>%
  filter(!is.na(OS_time), !is.na(OS_event), OS_time >= 0)

dim(clinical_surv)
## [1] 460  92

7 7. Kaplan-Meier global y por sexo

7.1 7.1 Kaplan-Meier global

km_fit_global <- survfit(Surv(OS_time, OS_event) ~ 1, data = clinical_surv)

km_global_plot <- ggsurvplot(
  km_fit_global,
  data = clinical_surv,
  conf.int = TRUE,
  risk.table = TRUE,
  censor = TRUE,
  xlab = "Tiempo (días)",
  ylab = "Probabilidad de supervivencia global",
  title = "Curva Kaplan-Meier global en TCGA-SKCM",
  ggtheme = theme_minimal()
)
## Ignoring unknown labels:
## • fill : "Strata"
km_global_plot
## Ignoring unknown labels:
## • fill : "Strata"
## Ignoring unknown labels:
## • fill : "Strata"
## Ignoring unknown labels:
## • fill : "Strata"
## Ignoring unknown labels:
## • colour : "Strata"

7.2 7.2 Kaplan-Meier por sexo

km_fit_gender <- survfit(Surv(OS_time, OS_event) ~ gender, data = clinical_surv)

km_gender_plot <- ggsurvplot(
  km_fit_gender,
  data = clinical_surv,
  conf.int = TRUE,
  pval = TRUE,
  risk.table = TRUE,
  censor = TRUE,
  xlab = "Tiempo (días)",
  ylab = "Probabilidad de supervivencia global",
  title = "Curvas Kaplan-Meier por sexo",
  legend.title = "Sexo",
  ggtheme = theme_minimal()
)

km_gender_plot
## Ignoring unknown labels:
## • colour : "Sexo"

8 8. Kaplan-Meier por estadio tumoral

En esta versión se incorpora la estratificación por estadio AJCC, una variable clínica central en el pronóstico del melanoma.

8.1 8.1 Exploración de niveles de estadio

table(clinical_surv$ajcc_pathologic_stage, useNA = "ifany")
## 
##    Stage 0    Stage I   Stage IA   Stage IB   Stage II  Stage IIA  Stage IIB 
##          6         30         18         29         30         18         28 
##  Stage IIC  Stage III Stage IIIA Stage IIIB Stage IIIC   Stage IV       <NA> 
##         63         41         16         45         68         22         46
levels(clinical_surv$ajcc_pathologic_stage)
##  [1] "Stage 0"    "Stage I"    "Stage IA"   "Stage IB"   "Stage II"  
##  [6] "Stage IIA"  "Stage IIB"  "Stage IIC"  "Stage III"  "Stage IIIA"
## [11] "Stage IIIB" "Stage IIIC" "Stage IV"

8.2 8.2 Limpieza de estadio para análisis

Algunos niveles tienen pocos casos o representan categorías no informativas para el análisis principal. Aquí eliminamos valores perdidos y mantenemos las categorías con información clínica interpretable.

clinical_stage <- clinical_surv %>%
  filter(!is.na(ajcc_pathologic_stage))

dim(clinical_stage)
## [1] 414  92
table(clinical_stage$ajcc_pathologic_stage)
## 
##    Stage 0    Stage I   Stage IA   Stage IB   Stage II  Stage IIA  Stage IIB 
##          6         30         18         29         30         18         28 
##  Stage IIC  Stage III Stage IIIA Stage IIIB Stage IIIC   Stage IV 
##         63         41         16         45         68         22

8.3 8.3 Kaplan-Meier por estadio detallado

km_fit_stage <- survfit(Surv(OS_time, OS_event) ~ ajcc_pathologic_stage, data = clinical_stage)

summary(km_fit_stage)
## Call: survfit(formula = Surv(OS_time, OS_event) ~ ajcc_pathologic_stage, 
##     data = clinical_stage)
##

8.4 8.4 Gráfico Kaplan-Meier por estadio detallado

km_stage_plot <- ggsurvplot(
  km_fit_stage,
  data = clinical_stage,
  conf.int = FALSE,
  pval = TRUE,
  risk.table = TRUE,
  censor = TRUE,
  xlab = "Tiempo (días)",
  ylab = "Probabilidad de supervivencia global",
  title = "Curvas Kaplan-Meier por estadio AJCC",
  legend.title = "Estadio",
  ggtheme = theme_minimal()
)

km_stage_plot
## Ignoring unknown labels:
## • colour : "Estadio"

8.5 8.5 Test log-rank por estadio

logrank_stage <- survdiff(Surv(OS_time, OS_event) ~ ajcc_pathologic_stage, data = clinical_stage)

logrank_stage
## Call:
## survdiff(formula = Surv(OS_time, OS_event) ~ ajcc_pathologic_stage, 
##     data = clinical_stage)
## 
##                                   N Observed Expected (O-E)^2/E (O-E)^2/V
## ajcc_pathologic_stage=Stage 0     6        5     5.21   0.00848   0.00881
## ajcc_pathologic_stage=Stage I    30       17    30.88   6.23629   7.89496
## ajcc_pathologic_stage=Stage IA   18        6    10.46   1.89875   2.01700
## ajcc_pathologic_stage=Stage IB   29       16    19.32   0.57191   0.63831
## ajcc_pathologic_stage=Stage II   30       18    21.61   0.60343   0.68849
## ajcc_pathologic_stage=Stage IIA  18        8    10.53   0.60942   0.64797
## ajcc_pathologic_stage=Stage IIB  28       13    14.33   0.12331   0.14497
## ajcc_pathologic_stage=Stage IIC  63       20    12.75   4.12913   4.57269
## ajcc_pathologic_stage=Stage III  41       18    27.02   3.01007   3.52984
## ajcc_pathologic_stage=Stage IIIA 16       12     7.64   2.48218   2.59723
## ajcc_pathologic_stage=Stage IIIB 45       21    15.12   2.29104   2.53876
## ajcc_pathologic_stage=Stage IIIC 68       33    17.02  15.01245  16.98837
## ajcc_pathologic_stage=Stage IV   22       10     5.12   4.65286   4.81979
## 
##  Chisq= 47.1  on 12 degrees of freedom, p= 4e-06

9 9. Agrupación clínica opcional de estadios

Para facilitar la interpretación, puede ser útil agrupar subestadios en categorías más amplias.

9.1 9.1 Creación de grupos simplificados

clinical_stage <- clinical_stage %>%
  mutate(
    stage_group = case_when(
      ajcc_pathologic_stage %in% c("Stage 0", "Stage I", "Stage IA", "Stage IB") ~ "Stage I",
      ajcc_pathologic_stage %in% c("Stage II", "Stage IIA", "Stage IIB", "Stage IIC") ~ "Stage II",
      ajcc_pathologic_stage %in% c("Stage III", "Stage IIIA", "Stage IIIB", "Stage IIIC") ~ "Stage III",
      ajcc_pathologic_stage %in% c("Stage IV") ~ "Stage IV",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

clinical_stage$stage_group <- factor(
  clinical_stage$stage_group,
  levels = c("Stage I", "Stage II", "Stage III", "Stage IV")
)

table(clinical_stage$stage_group, useNA = "ifany")
## 
##   Stage I  Stage II Stage III  Stage IV 
##        83       139       170        22

9.2 9.2 Kaplan-Meier por grupos simplificados

km_fit_stage_group <- survfit(Surv(OS_time, OS_event) ~ stage_group, data = clinical_stage)

summary(km_fit_stage_group)

9.3 9.3 Gráfico Kaplan-Meier por grupos simplificados

km_stage_group_plot <- ggsurvplot(
  km_fit_stage_group,
  data = clinical_stage,
  conf.int = FALSE,
  pval = TRUE,
  risk.table = TRUE,
  censor = TRUE,
  xlab = "Tiempo (días)",
  ylab = "Probabilidad de supervivencia global",
  title = "Curvas Kaplan-Meier por grupos de estadio",
  legend.title = "Grupo de estadio",
  ggtheme = theme_minimal()
)

km_stage_group_plot
## Ignoring unknown labels:
## • colour : "Grupo de estadio"

9.4 9.4 Test log-rank por grupos simplificados

logrank_stage_group <- survdiff(Surv(OS_time, OS_event) ~ stage_group, data = clinical_stage)

logrank_stage_group
## Call:
## survdiff(formula = Surv(OS_time, OS_event) ~ stage_group, data = clinical_stage)
## 
##                         N Observed Expected (O-E)^2/E (O-E)^2/V
## stage_group=Stage I    83       44    65.87  7.259366  11.36571
## stage_group=Stage II  139       59    59.22  0.000815   0.00119
## stage_group=Stage III 170       84    66.79  4.432068   6.94505
## stage_group=Stage IV   22       10     5.12  4.652857   4.81979
## 
##  Chisq= 17.2  on 3 degrees of freedom, p= 6e-04

10 10. Guardado de resultados

saveRDS(clinical_stage, "data_processed/tcga_skcm_clinical_stage_survival.rds")
saveRDS(km_fit_stage, "data_processed/km_fit_stage.rds")
saveRDS(km_fit_stage_group, "data_processed/km_fit_stage_group.rds")

ggsave(
  "figures/km_stage_plot.png",
  plot = km_stage_plot$plot,
  width = 9,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  "figures/km_stage_group_plot.png",
  plot = km_stage_group_plot$plot,
  width = 9,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300
)

11 11. Interpretación inicial

La estratificación por estadio tumoral permite evaluar si la supervivencia global difiere entre categorías AJCC, algo esperable desde el punto de vista clínico en melanoma. Este análisis es importante porque:

  • valida que la cohorte reproduce patrones clínicos conocidos,
  • permite comprobar que las variables de supervivencia han sido construidas correctamente,
  • y establece una referencia clínica antes de incorporar biomarcadores moleculares.

El análisis por grupos simplificados también será útil en las siguientes fases del TFM, especialmente al construir modelos de Cox con covariables clínicas.
